page: Microsoft Azure F64as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Fasv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The configuration features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core allocation. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 32 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 (specifically avx512f, avx512_vnni, and avx512_bf16), AES, VAES, and SHA_ni cryptography extensions.

This server operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, but it is Premium Storage capable, requiring remote storage attachments for persistent data. The lack of local NVMe or SSD storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, though it supports high-performance external storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the F64as_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as the PassMark CPU and physics tests. Memory performance is strong, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching the top 10% at 9339.97 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write speeds rank in the top 25%. Multi-threaded decompression and software compilation benchmarks also rank in the top 10%. For database and web workloads, the server achieves top-tier results in Redis SET operations and strong throughput in static web serving. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing for small models and text generation for medium models, though text generation for larger models drops to average or strong tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical core allocation and high clock speed deliver high compute density per resource unit, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks without the overhead of hyperthreading. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio balances raw processing power with sufficient memory capacity. This server is suited for multi-threaded software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, database operations, and medium-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v6 is a compute-focused server from the Fasv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a 1:4 memory ratio, providing 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. In benchmarks, the F64as_v6 delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. Its dedicated core allocation ensures consistent compute performance. This server is suited for CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v6 is a 64-vCPU server powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor and 256 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance for intensive compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v6 features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU performance for compute-heavy enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC processor platform delivering top-tier multi-threaded compute performance and balanced memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, software compilation, and physics benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses
Suited for multi-threaded compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference
